prompt: A scientist initially measures 9,000 bacteria in a growth medium. 10 hours later, the scientist measures 18,000 bacteria. Assuming exponential growth, the formula $P = C(2)^{rt}$ gives the number of bacteria in the growth medium, where $r$ and $C$ are constants and $P$ is the number of bacteria $t$ hours after the initial measurement. What is the value of $r$?
questionText: A scientist initially measures 9,000 bacteria in a growth medium. 10 hours later, the scientist measures 18,000 bacteria. questionText: The predicted number, $B(t)$, of a certain type of bacteria cell in a growth medium is given by the function $B(t) = 80(2)^t$, where $t$ is the time, in days, since the number of bacteria cells was first measured. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $B(4) = 1,280$ in this context?

choices.text: 4 days after the number of bacteria cells was first measured, the predicted number of bacteria cells was 1,280.
4 days after the number of bacteria cells was first measured, the predicted number of bacteria cells was 16.
1,280 days after the number of bacteria cells was first measured, the predicted number of bacteria cells was 4.
1,280 days after the number of bacteria cells was first measured, the predicted number of bacteria cells was 16.

questionText: The graph models the number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of every six-month period, where $x$ is the number of six-month periods since the end of January 1995 and $0 \leq x \leq 4$. Which statement is the best interpretation of the point $(1, 500)$ in this context?

choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of the first six-month period was 500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers increased every six months by 500 subscribers.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1995 was 500.
The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January was 500.

$f(x) = 7,000(1.005)^{2x}$

choices.text: Every 6 months, the balance increases by about \$5.
Every 2 months, the balance increases by about \$5.
At the end of every 6-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 6-month interval.
At the end of every 2-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 2-month interval.
